findings.description: The NC Waste Management logo appears in the HTML but is rendered smaller/differently styled compared to the PDF original
findings.evidence: PDF shows a larger NC logo with 'Waste Management ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY' beneath it. HTML shows a similar but slightly different rendering of the same logo.

findings.description: The header information layout is significantly different. In the PDF, the official names (PAT MCCRORY Governor, DONALD R. VAN DER VAART Secretary, MICHAEL SCOTT Acting Director) are right-aligned in the header area. In the HTML, they are left-aligned below the logo, and the address/contact info appears as a separate block before the memorandum.
findings.evidence: PDF: Right-aligned header with governor, secretary, and acting director names. HTML: Left-aligned block with the same names, plus address information appears between the header and the MEMORANDUM heading.

findings.description: The footer contact information from the PDF page bottom is repositioned to appear in the header area of the HTML
findings.evidence: PDF: 'State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Waste Management' and address at page bottom. HTML: Same info appears between header names and MEMORANDUM heading.

findings.description: The outline items (I-IV) in the HTML are rendered as clickable hyperlinks, while in the PDF they are plain text
findings.evidence: PDF: 'I. History/Background' as plain text. HTML: 'I. History/Background' as a colored hyperlink.

findings.description: Page headers with report title, location, date, and page numbers present in PDF are absent in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF pages 2-5 have right-aligned headers reading 'Meeting Officer's Report / Coal Ash Impoundment Classification(s) / G.G. Allen Steam Station, Gaston County / April 22, 2016 / Page X of 5'. HTML has no such headers.

findings.description: The digital signature information appears at the top of the HTML content but is not visible on the PDF pages
findings.evidence: HTML shows 'Digitally signed by LYF DN: cn=LYF, o=DWM, ou=SWS, email=larry.frost@ncdenr.gov, c=US Date: 2016.05.12 12:11:52 -04:00' at the top. This is not visible in the PDF page images.

findings.description: Fabricated navigation links in the table of contents. The PDF's outline is a simple ordered list without hyperlinks, but the HTML adds internal anchor links (<a href="#history-background">, etc.) that do not exist in the source document.
findings.evidence:
PDF shows plain text: 'I. History/Background' etc. HTML adds: 'History/Background' with a
wrapper. These are fabricated interactive elements.

findings.description: A mailto link was fabricated for the email address in the digital signature block. The PDF shows the email as plain text within the digital signature metadata, but the HTML wraps it in a clickable mailto link.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'email=larry.frost@ncdenr.gov' (part of digital signature info). HTML: 'larry.frost@ncdenr.gov'

findings.description: The document title 'MEMORANDUM' is used as h1, which is acceptable. However, the table of contents is wrapped in a <nav> element which was not in the PDF structure. This is a minor structural addition but does not misrepresent the document.
findings.evidence: HTML adds <nav aria-label="Table of contents"> wrapper around the outline list.

findings.description: The memorandum header information (To, From, Subject, date, meeting description, and outline) appears on both page 1 of the PDF. The HTML faithfully reproduces this once, so no actual duplication issue exists. However, the PDF repeats the From/Subject block content on page 1 (cover page) and again on page 1 (signed version), and the HTML consolidates them into one block, which is acceptable.
findings.evidence: Page 1 of PDF has two renderings of the memo header (unsigned letterhead version and signed version). HTML presents one consolidated version.

findings.description: Minor inconsistency in attachment list numbering between Section III (Written Public Comment Summary) and Section IV (Attachments). The PDF body text references 'Attachment V-Supporting Documentation' and 'Attachment VI-USPS Comment' but in the Attachments list, items V and VI are swapped in description. The HTML faithfully reproduces this inconsistency from the PDF, so this is not an HTML error.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML: Section III says 'Attachment V-Supporting Documentation' and 'Attachment VI-USPS Comment'. Section IV lists 'V. Written Public Comments Received' and 'VI. Supporting documentation received during public hearing' - this inconsistency exists in the source PDF.
